gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 high-reasoning review found one P2: restaurant_kv_serving compatibility configs inherited the new pip package fields but bypassed their validation in overridden __post_init__ methods. Fixed by mirroring pip package validation and tuple coercion for legacy target, matrix, and single-job configs, routing the helper through the isolated document namespace, and adding legacy regression tests. Delta review approved with no findings.
verification: PYTHONPATH=src pytest tests/test_databricks_engine_probe_job.py -q (75 passed); PYTHONPATH=src pytest tests/test_databricks_engine_probe_job.py tests/test_benchmark_plan.py tests/test_benchmark_plan_executor.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py -q (264 passed); python -m compileall -q src tests; git diff --check; PYTHONPATH=src pytest -q (1391 passed)
what_changed: Databricks engine-probe jobs can now install PyPI runtime package specs before local wheel artifacts, and backend target JSON can carry per-backend pip_packages for incompatible vLLM/SGLang runtime stacks.
why: Strict native engine-probe evidence on target g6/L4 Databricks tasks needs real vLLM and SGLang runtimes installed before Cachet and adapter wheels load. The previous runner could install wheels only, which blocked release-safe native probe runs from a self-contained target file.
